_note: Original Practice questions aligned with CS Navigator's original Learn lesson for this topic.

questions.title: Enums practice 1
questions.prompt: Which assignment is valid for this enum?
choices: Level level = Level.MEDIUM;
Level level = "MEDIUM";
Level level = 2;
Level level = new Level();
questions.explanation: An enum variable holds one of its declared constants. The constant is reached through the enum type, so Level.MEDIUM is the valid value.

questions.title: Enums practice 2
choices: HIGH
Nothing
questions.explanation: The value is Level.HIGH, so the HIGH case runs and prints the text 3. The other cases are skipped.

questions.title: Trace Enums
questions.prompt: What output does this code produce? Enter each line in order.
questions.explanation: The variable can hold one Status constant. Java checks the name during compilation. The resulting output is 'ACTIVE'.

questions.title: Enums good practice
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Enums?
choices: Use an enum for a fixed set of choices.
Skip input checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide every error and continue without checking the result.
Put all of the work in one long statement.
questions.explanation: Use an enum for a fixed set of choices. This keeps the code easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Enums practice 5
questions.prompt: Which version follows the lesson's safer approach?
choices: Status status = Status.ACTIVE;
String status = "acitve";
Both versions are equally safe.
Neither version can run.
questions.explanation: Strings can contain an unnoticed spelling difference. The enum version is checked by the compiler and gives the value a clear type.

questions.title: Enums practice 6
questions.prompt: Which statement best explains 'Changing text into an enum can fail'?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
`Status.valueOf(text)` requires an exact constant name. Input such as `"active"` does not match `ACTIVE` unless you clean or convert it first.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: `Status.valueOf(text)` requires an exact constant name. Input such as `"active"` does not match `ACTIVE` unless you clean or convert it first.

questions.title: Enums practice 7
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Enums?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Name constants clearly and consistently.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Name constants clearly and consistently. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Enums practice 8
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Enums?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
Store the enum value instead of a matching string.
questions.explanation: Store the enum value instead of a matching string. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Enums practice 9
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Enums?
choices: Handle outside text before calling `valueOf`.
Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Handle outside text before calling `valueOf`. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.

questions.title: Enums practice 10
questions.prompt: Which is a useful practice for Enums?
choices: Skip checks and assume every value is valid.
Use switch when each constant needs different behavior.
Hide errors and continue without checking the result.
Put every step into one long statement.
questions.explanation: Use switch when each constant needs different behavior. This makes the result easier to understand and verify.
